metadata.summary: API tests are organized in a pyramid: many unit tests, some integration tests against TestClient/httpx, targeted contract tests (Pact) only across team or service boundaries, and few E2E tests. Every integration test must validate the response body against the OpenAPI schema — status code alone is not enough.

content.title: API Testing
content.summary: API tests are organized in a pyramid: many unit tests, some integration tests against TestClient/httpx, targeted contract tests (Pact) only across team or service boundaries, and few E2E tests. Every integration test must validate the response body against the OpenAPI schema — status code alone is not enough. Snapshot openapi.json per PR and use oasdiff to classify breaking vs non-breaking changes.

why.p: Most regressions are schema drift — a field disappears, a type changes, a required field becomes optional. Asserting only on status codes misses these. Generating tests from the served openapi.json (not a hand-maintained spec) keeps tests and implementation in sync. Contract tests (Pact) are justified only when consumer and provider are in separate repos or teams; for single-repo monoliths, an integration test covers the same surface at lower cost.

list.item: New REST/GraphQL service: generate happy-path + 4xx + 5xx tests from OpenAPI schema before implementation
After every spec change to openapi.yaml — regenerate validation tests to catch drift
Building integration tests with TestClient / httpx.AsyncClient for FastAPI or Django
Adding negative tests (auth failures, validation, rate-limiting, idempotency)
Frontend/backend contract is unstable across teams — drive Pact consumer tests from FE side
Pure unit-level logic with no transport or contract — plain pytest without schema validation
Greenfield prototypes where contract changes hourly — Pact pacts create churn faster than value
Single-team, single-repo monoliths where integration tests already cover the surface — contract tests are redundant
Pure browser flows (form clicks, redirects) — use Playwright/Cypress instead
Internal binary RPC with code-generated schemas — contract drift is impossible

rule.statement: Assert on the response body schema, not only the status code. A 200 with a missing required field is a regression.
rule.rationale: Schema drift is the most common API regression. Status code passes; body fails silently without schema assertion.

rule.statement: Generate tests from the served openapi.json (not a hand-maintained spec). Snapshot openapi.json per PR; use oasdiff to classify breaking vs non-breaking changes.
rule.rationale: A served spec is always in sync with the running app. A hand-maintained spec drifts within weeks.

rule.statement: Provide one auth fixture per role: anon_client, user_client, admin_client. Never thread tokens through test arguments.
rule.rationale: Consistent fixture names prevent duplicate auth setup across test files and make role boundaries explicit.

example.title: FastAPI Integration Test

rule.statement: Validate every response against the OpenAPI spec in integration tests. Use openapi-core; reject any test that only checks status_code.
rule.rationale: Status code is a coarse signal. Schema validation catches missing fields, type changes, and unexpected nullability.

example.title: OpenAPI Validation Example
example.code:
from openapi_core import OpenAPI from openapi_core.testing.mock import MockRequest, MockResponse
spec = OpenAPI.from_file_path("openapi.json")
def test_response_matches_spec(client): response = client.get("/users") mock_req = MockRequest("http://localhost", "GET", "/users") mock_res = MockResponse(data=response.content, status_code=response.status_code) result = spec.validate_response(mock_req, mock_res) assert not result.errors

rule.statement: For idempotent endpoints, call the operation twice in the same test to catch hidden side-effects.
rule.rationale: A second identical POST must return the same result (201 or 200) not a 500 due to constraint violation.

rule.statement: Require at least one failure-injection test for each dependent service: DB down (mock), third-party 503 (respx/WireMock).
rule.rationale: Happy-path tests pass even when error handling is completely missing. Failure injection surfaces missing try/except and missing fallback responses.

rule.statement: Use Pact contract tests only when consumer and provider are maintained by different teams or in separate repositories. For single-team single-repo monoliths, integration tests against TestClient cover the same surface at lower cost.
rule.rationale: Pact adds a broker, two-sided test setup, and a can-i-deploy gate. The overhead only pays off when independent deploy cycles exist.

example.title: Pact Consumer Setup (JavaScript)

rule.statement: Use pact-python v2 on the provider side. pactman is unmaintained — pin pact-python>=2.
rule.rationale: pactman has not had releases in years; pact-python v2 aligns with the Pact Specification v3/v4.

rule.statement: Consumer CI publishes pacts to the Pact Broker. Provider CI verifies against the latest consumer tag. Both sides run can-i-deploy before deploying to production.
rule.rationale: can-i-deploy prevents a provider from deploying a breaking change before all consumers have been updated.

rule.statement: Any change to openapi.json snapshot or a removed Pact pact must be reviewed by a human before merging.
rule.rationale: Agents will silently delete drifting tests to make CI green. Removing a pact means a consumer dependency was dropped — that is a product decision, not a test fix.

antipattern.description: Asserting only on status_code without validating the response body.
antipattern.reason: A 200 with a missing required field is a schema regression. Always validate body against the OpenAPI spec.

antipattern.description: Weakening assertions to fix a failing test (assert r.status_code in (200, 201, 204)).
antipattern.reason: Loosened assertions hide real regressions. Failures must be fixed in production code or the spec, not in the test assertion.

antipattern.description: Sharing test fixtures across parallel test runs with pytest-xdist without isolation.
antipattern.reason: Shared DB state causes non-deterministic failures. Use pytest.mark.serial or --dist=loadgroup for stateful tests.

antipattern.description: Hardcoding auth tokens or credentials in test fixtures and committing to git.
antipattern.reason: Leaked tokens are a security incident. Run a scrubber pass before every commit.

antipattern.description: Calling the live external service (Stripe, SendGrid) in tests because it works locally.
antipattern.reason: CI environments lack external credentials; tests become flaky. Mock at the HTTP boundary with respx/WireMock.

antipattern.description: Importing pactman on the provider side.
antipattern.reason: pactman is unmaintained. Use pact-python>=2 for all provider verification.

template.purpose: CI script: validate live responses against OpenAPI snapshot, fail on drift
